content:
How to Apply to Mangosuthu University of Technology (MUT)

Thinking about studying at Mangosuthu University of Technology (MUT)? Based in Durban (Umlazi), KwaZulu-Natal, MUT is a University of Technology established in 1979. This guide covers everything you need to know to apply successfully — from APS requirements to application deadlines, bursaries, and step-by-step instructions.

At a Glance: MUT Fast Facts

DetailInformation
Full NameMangosuthu University of Technology (MUT)
TypeUniversity of Technology
LocationUmlazi, Durban, KwaZulu-Natal
Year Founded1979
Application Portalhttps://www.mut.ac.za/index.php/admissions
Application FeeR100
Applications OpenApril 1
Applications CloseSeptember 30
Minimum APS18 (varies by programme)
Contact Phone031 907 7111
Contact Emailadmissions@mut.ac.za

Why Choose MUT?

Community-serving university of technology in South Durban.

  • Named after Mangosuthu Buthelezi, Chief Minister of KwaZulu and founder of the IFP
  • Established in 1979 to serve the Umlazi township, one of Africa's largest townships
  • Strong focus on engineering and natural sciences, serving the South Durban Basin's petrochemical industry
  • Unique in offering Nature Conservation as a flagship programme

Faculties and Programmes at MUT

MUT offers qualifications across 3 faculties:

  • Engineering
  • Management Sciences
  • Natural Sciences

Popular programmes include: ND: Mechanical Engineering, ND: Chemical Engineering, ND: Nature Conservation, ND: Supply Chain Management.

APS Requirements for MUT

The minimum Admission Point Score (APS) required at MUT is 18 points. However, individual programmes may require significantly higher scores:

APS range: 18–26 depending on programme

MUT has the most accessible entry requirements of the KZN universities of technology, with a minimum APS of 18 for National Diploma programmes. This reflects its mission to serve the Umlazi community.

How to Calculate Your APS

Your APS is calculated from your Grade 12 (NSC) subject results. Each subject receives a score from 1 to 7 based on your percentage:

Percentage (%)Rating CodeAPS Points
80–100%7 (Outstanding)7
70–79%6 (Meritorious)6
60–69%5 (Substantial)5
50–59%4 (Adequate)4
40–49%3 (Moderate)3
30–39%2 (Elementary)2
0–29%1 (Not Achieved)1

Your APS is the sum of your six best subjects (excluding Life Orientation, which is counted at half-value at most universities, or excluded entirely).

Use our free APS Calculator to calculate your score instantly.

Step-by-Step: How to Apply to MUT

Step 1: Check Programme Requirements

Before applying, confirm the specific APS requirements and subject prerequisites for your chosen programme at https://www.mut.ac.za. Some programmes require specific subjects (e.g., Mathematics for Engineering, Life Sciences for Medicine).

Step 2: Gather Your Documents

You will typically need:

  • Certified copy of your ID (South African ID or passport for international students)
  • Certified copy of your Grade 11 final results (if applying before matric)
  • Certified copy of your NSC / Grade 12 results (final matric results when available)
  • Proof of residence (e.g., utility bill)
  • Completed application form (online or paper)

Step 3: Apply Online

Visit the MUT online application portal at https://www.mut.ac.za/index.php/admissions. Create an account, complete the application form, upload your documents, and pay the application fee of R100.

Step 4: Submit Before the Deadline

Applications for 2027 admission typically open in April 1 and close on September 30. Apply as early as possible — popular programmes fill up quickly, especially Medicine, Law, and Engineering.

Step 5: Accept Your Offer

Once you receive a conditional or unconditional offer from MUT, accept it within the specified timeframe (usually 2–4 weeks). You may need to pay a registration deposit to secure your place.

Step 6: Register for Your Programme

After accepting your offer, complete the formal registration process in January (or mid-year for Semester 2 programmes). Bring original and certified copies of all documents.

Application Deadlines for MUT

Missing the application deadline is one of the most common reasons students don't get into their chosen institution. Here are the key dates:

  • Applications Open: April 1
  • Application Deadline: September 30
  • Results Notification: Typically November–December for the following year
  • Registration: January (Semester 1) or mid-year

Pro tip: Don't wait until the last day. Apply when applications open — some programmes are filled on a first-come, first-served basis.

Bursaries and Financial Aid at MUT

Studying at MUT can be financed through several bursaries and financial aid options:

  • NSFAS
  • MUT Financial Aid
  • SAPREF Refinery Bursary
  • KZN Provincial Bursaries

NSFAS (National Student Financial Aid Scheme): If your combined household income is below R350,000 per year, you may qualify for NSFAS funding. Apply at www.nsfas.org.za from October each year for the following academic year.

Preparing for MUT While Still in School

The best time to prepare for university is while you're still in Grade 10, 11, or 12. Here's what you can do:

  • Focus on your key subjects: Make sure you're strong in the subjects required for your chosen programme
  • Use AI tutoring: StudyBuddy's AI tutor helps you master difficult CAPS topics before matric
  • Research your options early: Visit open days at MUT to understand campus life
  • Apply for bursaries early: Some bursaries (like Allan Gray, Anglo American) are available while you're still in Grade 11
  • Track your APS: Use our APS calculator to know where you stand

What Happens After You Apply?

After submitting your application to MUT, the process typically follows these stages:

  1. Application Confirmation: You'll receive an email or SMS confirming receipt of your application
  2. Conditional Offer: If your Grade 11 results meet the requirements, you may receive a conditional offer
  3. Final Results Submission: Submit your final NSC results in January
  4. Unconditional Offer: If your matric results meet or exceed the requirements, your offer becomes unconditional
  5. Registration: Register for your programme and pay your fees or confirm NSFAS/bursary funding

Comparing MUT With Other Universities

Not sure if MUT is the right fit? Here are some things to consider:

FactorMUTTypical Alternative
TypeUniversity of TechnologyTraditional University / UoT
LocationDurban (Umlazi)Varies
Minimum APS1820–37 (varies widely)
Application FeeR100R100–R400
NSFAS EligibleYesYes (public) / No (private)

Frequently Asked Questions About Applying to MUT

Still have questions? Our FAQ section below answers the most common questions about applying to MUT.

Need extra help with your Grade 12 subjects to meet MUT's entry requirements? StudyBuddy's AI tutor is FREE to start — get personalised help in Mathematics, Sciences, English, and all CAPS subjects. Most students improve their marks by 20–30% within a few weeks.
keywords: Mangosuthu University of Technology (MUT) how to apply
quickAnswer: To apply to Mangosuthu University of Technology (MUT), visit https://www.mut.ac.za/index.php/admissions, pay the R100 application fee, and submit your documents before September 30. Minimum APS is 18 for most programmes.

faqs.question: What is the minimum APS to apply to MUT?
faqs.answer: The minimum APS at MUT is 18 points for most programmes. However, competitive programmes like Medicine, Law, or Engineering may require significantly higher scores (18–26 depending on programme). Always check the specific requirements for your chosen programme on the MUT website.

faqs.question: When do applications open for MUT?
faqs.answer: Applications for MUT typically open in April 1 and close on September 30. We strongly recommend applying as early as possible, as popular programmes fill up quickly. Visit https://www.mut.ac.za/index.php/admissions to apply online.

faqs.question: How much is the application fee for MUT?
faqs.answer: The application fee at MUT is R100. Some students may qualify for a fee waiver — check the MUT website for details. The application fee is usually non-refundable even if your application is unsuccessful.

faqs.question: Does MUT accept NSFAS?
faqs.answer: Yes, MUT is an accredited NSFAS institution. If your household income is below R350,000 per year, you may qualify for NSFAS funding which can cover tuition, accommodation, meals, transport, and a living allowance. Apply at www.nsfas.org.za from October each year.

faqs.question: What documents do I need to apply to MUT?
faqs.answer: For your application to MUT, you will need: a certified copy of your ID, certified copies of your Grade 11 and/or Grade 12 results, proof of residence, and any programme-specific requirements (portfolio for arts, HPAT scores for health sciences, etc.). All documents should be certified within 3 months of the application date.
